introSection: Runaway Mine Train is an Arrow Development roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. It was the park's first steel coaster when it opened with the park in 1974.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Runaway Mine Train opened in July 1974, along with the park.

The overhaul of Runaway Mine Train in 1995 was the first project that Larry Chickola completed for Six Flags. He redesigned the ride's motor, cars and computer operating system.

For the 2006 season, Runaway Mine Train's track was repainted with brighter colors and the trains were fitted with new individual lap bar restraints. However, problems with these restraints caused several lengthy periods of downtime throughout the season due to malfunctions in the trains' locking systems.

The park partnered with RWJBarnabas Health and Rutgers Cancer Institute of New Jersey in 2019 when they repainted one of the trains to include hand prints of cancer survivors and supporters.

fullText:
Runaway Mine Train is an Arrow Development roller coaster located at Six Flags Great Adventure in Jackson, New Jersey, USA. It was the park's first steel coaster when it opened with the park in 1974.

Runaway Mine Train opened in July 1974, along with the park.

The overhaul of Runaway Mine Train in 1995 was the first project that Larry Chickola completed for Six Flags. He redesigned the ride's motor, cars and computer operating system.

For the 2006 season, Runaway Mine Train's track was repainted with brighter colors and the trains were fitted with new individual lap bar restraints. However, problems with these restraints caused several lengthy periods of downtime throughout the season due to malfunctions in the trains' locking systems.

The park partnered with RWJBarnabas Health and Rutgers Cancer Institute of New Jersey in 2019 when they repainted one of the trains to include hand prints of cancer survivors and supporters.



Red track and supports.
